title: Aureline adapter-drift event contract
description: Boundary schema for the adapter_drift_event_record every consumer (run / test / build / debug picker, quick-open, command palette, problems pane, output pane header, AI action sheet, CI overlay, support-bundle exporter) reads when an active build adapter's capability surface changes shape: a capability is added or removed, the active adapter's confidence ceiling shifts, target ids re-resolve under a different adapter, freshness drops, partial coverage opens or closes, or the workspace falls back to opaque preservation. Companion to schemas/tooling/adapter_descriptor.schema.json (the closed adapter / capability / precedence vocabularies the prior and current states cite), schemas/tooling/target_graph_snapshot.schema.json (graph_id, partial_truth_class, freshness_class), schemas/tooling/target_descriptor.schema.json (target_id, four typed *_action_support descriptors), and schemas/tooling/fallback_confidence.schema.json (the fallback-confidence record drift events emit). Companion narrative: docs/tooling/fallback_confidence_and_adapter_drift_contract.md. The record explicitly names what changed (the capability delta), who was affected (target ids and run / test / build / debug action classes), and how consumer surfaces MUST disclose the change (a closed required-behavior set so capability loss never collapses into a generic not_available state). Raw URLs, raw absolute paths, raw command lines, raw secret values, raw env bodies, raw lockfile contents, and raw stdout / stderr never appear in any payload here. Adding a new drift_class, capability_change_class, required_disclosure_behavior_class, or affected_action_class is additive-minor and bumps adapter_drift_event_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

adapter_drift_event_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the adapter-drift-event contract. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields and new enum members do not bump this version.

opaque_id.description: Opaque stable identifier safe to log and export. Raw secrets, raw absolute paths, raw URLs, raw command lines, and raw payload bytes never appear here.

drift_class.description: Closed drift-class vocabulary. capability_upgrade_admitted_* classes carry no required user admission step but still emit a visible chip so AI planners and CI overlays render the change. capability_downgrade_disclosed_* and adapter_unavailable_no_replacement and fallback_to_opaque_preservation MUST narrow affordances immediately and cite the adapter class plus reason. adapter_swap_visible_state_change pairs with an adapter_replacement_event_record in schemas/tooling/adapter_descriptor.schema.json.

capability_change_class.description: Closed capability-change vocabulary. Names how a single capability flag changed across the drift event. Demotion classes MUST narrow affordances immediately; promotion classes MAY widen affordances without further user admission but still emit a visible chip.

capability_change_entry.description: One capability change entry. The drift event MUST list every changed capability so a single drift event never silently drops a capability change off the report.

required_disclosure_behavior_class.description: Closed required-disclosure vocabulary. Pickers, problems pane, output pane header, AI action sheets, CI overlays, and support-bundle exporters read this set and render exactly the listed disclosures so capability loss is uniformly visible.

adapter_id_ref.description: Ref to the adapter_descriptor_record (in schemas/tooling/adapter_descriptor.schema.json) the snapshot resolves through.

publishing_graph_id_ref.description: Ref to the target_graph_snapshot_record graph_id this adapter most recently published under the workset_scope. MAY be the same as the prior or current snapshot's graph_id depending on whether the drift event also rotates the graph.

workset_scope_ref.description: Optional ref to the workset_scope object in schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json the drift event was resolved under. Null only when the drift is workspace-wide (for example adapter_unavailable_no_replacement before any scope has been resolved).

changed_capability_set.description: Set of capability changes the drift event introduces. MUST be non-empty: a drift event without a capability change is a no-op.

affected_target_id_refs.description: Refs to target_descriptor_record target_ids the drift narrows or widens. Empty when the drift is workspace-wide; non-empty when the drift narrows specific targets.

affected_action_class_set.description: Set of run / test / build / debug actions affected by the drift. Empty when the drift does not narrow specific actions.

required_disclosure_behavior_class_set.description: Set of disclosure behaviors consumer surfaces MUST apply when this drift event is rendered. MUST include at least one in-product chip class so capability loss is visible to the user, not only to support.

drift_reason_class.description: Closed drift-reason vocabulary. Every drift event MUST cite a typed reason so capability loss names the cause rather than collapsing into a generic not_available state. no_reason_admissible_review_only is reserved for drift events whose only honest disclosure is opening a review thread for support to investigate.

linked_adapter_replacement_event_id_ref.description: Optional ref to the adapter_replacement_event_record (in schemas/tooling/adapter_descriptor.schema.json) the drift event pairs with. MUST be non-null when drift_class is adapter_swap_visible_state_change.

linked_fallback_confidence_record_id_ref.description: Optional ref to the fallback_confidence_record (in schemas/tooling/fallback_confidence.schema.json) emitted alongside the drift event. MUST be non-null when drift_class is fallback_to_opaque_preservation, adapter_unavailable_no_replacement, or any capability_downgrade_disclosed_* class.

lifecycle_status_card_ref.description: Optional ref to a lifecycle_status_card_record (in schemas/ux/lifecycle_status_card.schema.json) the drift event fans out to. MUST be non-null when required_disclosure_behavior_class_set contains emit_lifecycle_status_card_for_workspace_or_target.

adapter_drift_event_record.description: One adapter-drift event. Pickers, quick-open, command palette, problems pane, output pane header, AI action sheets, CI overlays, and support-bundle exporters read this record so capability shape changes are visible, traceable, and recoverable.

allOf.description: adapter_swap_visible_state_change drift class MUST cite a non-null linked_adapter_replacement_event_id_ref.
fallback_to_opaque_preservation, adapter_unavailable_no_replacement, and capability_downgrade_disclosed_* drift classes MUST cite a non-null linked_fallback_confidence_record_id_ref.
fallback_to_opaque_preservation drift class MUST set current_adapter_state.adapter_class to fallback_opaque_preservation and current_adapter_state.adapter_confidence_ceiling to unknown.
capability_downgrade_disclosed_* drift classes MUST include emit_visible_state_change_chip_in_picker in required_disclosure_behavior_class_set so the picker narrows affordances immediately.
fallback_to_opaque_preservation and adapter_unavailable_no_replacement drift classes MUST include emit_lifecycle_status_card_for_workspace_or_target in required_disclosure_behavior_class_set.
required_disclosure_behavior_class_set MUST always include emit_support_export_disclosure_with_capability_loss_summary so capability loss survives a support bundle export.

current_adapter_state.adapter_confidence_ceiling MUST NOT exceed prior_adapter_state.adapter_confidence_ceiling on capability_downgrade_disclosed_lower_confidence and fallback_to_opaque_preservation drift classes.
